2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called offline_and_remove_memory_ranges() to the Linux kernel's memory_hotplug module. The function takes an array of memory ranges and processes them as one operation under a single lock, ensuring atomicity and resolving a race condition between system daemons trying to manage the same resource. It first offlines every block in all ranges, then removes the ranges only if all are offline, reverting the whole operation if any offline fails. This provides callers with all-or-nothing semantics for the offline step, leaving the device in a consistent state even after a failed or interrupted unplug. The existing offline_and_remove_memory() function is updated to be a thin wrapper that passes its single range to the new helper.

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called offline_and_remove_memory_ranges() to the Linux kernel's memory_hotplug module. The function takes an array of memory ranges and processes them as one operation under a single lock, ensuring atomicity and resolving a race condition between system daemons trying to manage the same resource. It first offlines every block in all ranges, then removes the ranges only if all are offline, reverting the whole operation if any offline fails. This provides callers with all-or-nothing semantics for the offline step, leaving the device in a consistent state even in case of failure or interruption. The patch also updates the existing offline_and_remove_memory() function to be a thin wrapper that passes its single range to the new helper.
